title: Dactylorchis kerryensis
intro:
I couldn't find any information about the plant Dactylorchis kerryensis. However, I found information about the plant Dactylorhiza kerryensis (also known as Dactylorhiza maculata ssp. kerryensis).

Here's a paragraph about Dactylorhiza kerryensis:

Dactylorhiza kerryensis, also known as Kerry Orchid or Spotted Orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various health issues such as wounds, infections, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorhiza kerryensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Dactylorchis kerryensis belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This classification places Dactylorchis kerryensis within the broader group of orchids. Further classification of Dactylorchis kerryensis within the orchid family is as a member of the subtribe Orchidinae and the tribe Orchideae. However, it's worth noting that some classifications may group Dactylorchis kerryensis under the genus Neotinea, depending on the source. Dactylorchis kerryensis is also sometimes classified as Neotinea ustulata.
